name: append
description: Append the contents of one source file into a destination file after explicit confirmation. See #assets/payloads/FILEOPS/append.
outputs: Appends source file contents to destination file
Prints review, info, abort, error, and ok status messages

behavior.source_resolution: Uses $SRC if set, otherwise falls back to $1
behavior.destination_resolution: Uses $DEST if set, otherwise falls back to $2
behavior.confirmation_gate: Requires the user to type yes before writing
behavior.validation: Fails if the source file does not exist

notes: Designed for explicit file-to-file append operations
Suitable for controlled merges and manual review workflows

name: catappend
description: Interactively append multi-line stdin content to a target file, with file selection fallback and append-only behavior. See #assets/payloads/FILEOPS/catappend.
outputs: Appends captured stdin to the chosen target file
Prints start, separator, and done messages

behavior.target_resolution: Uses $1 first, then fzf selection, then manual filename prompt
behavior.capture_mode: Reads stdin directly and appends it to the target file
behavior.safety: Never truncates or overwrites the target file
fallbacks: fzf selection when no filename is supplied
manual prompt when fzf returns nothing

notes: Requires an interactive terminal for the prompt flow
Best used when adding content to an existing file

name: daudit
description: Grid-style directory audit that reports directory names and sizes in MB, with optional max-depth control. See #assets/payloads/DIRECTORIES/daudit.

prompts: Enter max depth (Press Enter for Infinity)

outputs: Formats each line with a directory icon

behavior.help_mode: Shows usage and explanation when invoked with -h or no argument
behavior.depth_control: Uses -maxdepth only when the user enters a value
behavior.scan_mode: Recursively finds directories under the target path
behavior.rendering: Prints a directory icon and the human-readable size

notes: The script computes the basename but does not currently print it
The commented gridify pipeline suggests future column formatting

name: faudit
description: Live-stream a file audit for a directory, optionally limited by max depth, with extension-based icons and color-coded output. See #assets/payloads/FILEOPS/faudit.

prompts: Enter max depth (Press Enter for Infinity)

outputs: Lists files discovered under the target directory

behavior.help_mode: Shows usage and a short explanation when no directory or -h is provided
behavior.depth_control: Applies -maxdepth only when the user supplies a value

notes: Output depends on terminal support for emoji and ANSI colors

name: regfind
description: Recursive regex search across a path using grep with line numbers and binary-file suppression. See #assets/payloads/SYSTEM/regfind.
outputs: Searches recursively through the selected path

behavior.validation: Exits with usage text if the regex is missing
behavior.error_handling: Suppresses stderr from inaccessible paths with 2>/dev/null
behavior.safety: Read-only search; does not modify files

notes: Best for fast codebase or filesystem text searches
The default path is the current directory

name: rename-to-md
description: Batch-rename every non-directory file in the current directory by appending the .md extension. See #assets/payloads/TEMPLATES/rename-to-md.
outputs: Renames each file to add a .md suffix

behavior.scan_mode: Iterates over all entries matched by * in the current directory
behavior.rename_rule: Moves file to the same name plus .md
behavior.overwrite_behavior: Uses mv and therefore follows the shell/OS rename semantics
behavior.safety: Modifies filenames in place; no content changes

notes: The script is a template for bulk rename workflows
Because it uses * directly, it only targets the current directory

name: shell-audit
description: Capture a shell/environment audit into a timestamped Markdown log, including active shell, user dotfiles, and Termux system shell files. See #assets/payloads/SYSTEM/shell-audit.
outputs: Creates a timestamped Markdown log under Obsidian/LOGS/shell
Prints the current shell and process name

behavior.logging: Captures stdout and stderr into a timestamped Markdown file
behavior.scope: Audits user shell setup and Termux shell-related configuration
behavior.execution_model: Runs an embedded bash script inside bash and tees output
behavior.safety: Read-only inspection; no file writes except the generated log

notes: Useful for diagnosing shell initialization issues
The embedded heredoc keeps the audit steps self-contained

name: termux-shell
description: Load a canonical Termux shell environment, source profile files and env.global, then hand control to bash. See #assets/payloads/FILEOPS/termux-shell.

behavior.tool_loading: Fails fast if env.global is missing
behavior.safety: No file writes; environment bootstrap only

notes: Designed for Termux sessions that need consistent shell initialization
Preserves interactive shell behavior before handing off to bash

title: Welcome to Infinity
preview: Learn how to use terminal helpers effectively.
